author: @cyberlizardcouk
text:
E-bikes.   Great idea but 250w!   Less power than some hand held power tools.  

They should get rid of the power rating and restrict them to speed only.  

If you live in a flat area they’re fine.  If you live anywhere with hills you may as well just walk or go in the car.

author: @tvbridge
text: P01....Brightness 1,2,3; P02....Km/m; P03....Voltage 32/48/60; P04....Sleep time minutes; P05....PAssist mode; P06....Wheel size inch; P07....Magnet number in motor; P08....Speed limit; P09....Start level of PAS; P10....2=Peddle & throttle Assist; P11....Boost sensitivity; P12....Boost start strength; P13....Number of magnets; P14....Current limit in Amps; P15....Low Voltage power off; P16....Clear milometer; P17....Cruise enable/disable; P18....Speed ratio % adjustment; P19....1=Direct Hub 2=Gear Hub; P20....Protocol=0000

author: @alibear57
text: Absolutely brilliantly explained, as always. Was just wondering if you would be able to expand on P18, 19 & 20 please? Other videos stated that P20 wasn't used, but mine has three settings 0, 1 & 2. Not knowing what ANY of them were for, I fiddled with each one in turn, and then regretted it, as my bike wouldn't work for hours - until I found a factory reset video. I got error codes E 08 & E 030 at different settings of P20. Don't know which setting got them to appear, or what I did to clear them, but they're gone now. Would be good to know what these three settings are for though. My bike is a Heybike Mars (I think - it looks identical to your A20), with an S866 controller. Cheers and thanks for another great video.

author: @eduardosilva7304
text:
Maybe it's useful to say that when you fold the bike, you have to do the trick again to unlock the throttle after unfolding it again. Takes only a few seconds, after getting use to the 4 steps, as Robert mencioned:
Throttle(1)/front brake(2)/back brake(3)/switch on the panel and wait 20 sec.(4). Then switch all off, before start to use it.
Thanks for the great tips and for being so precise on your explanations, this can be complicated for some of us!

author: @buddhahead9942
text:
P1 -screen brightness
P2 - km to miles
P3 - battery voltage
P4 auto sleep timer
P5 no of asisst 0/1/2 
P6 wheels diameter 
P7 no of magnets in the sensors
P8 speed cap (limit) 
P9 none zero start 
P10 drive mode 0 ( no assist) 1 ( electric assist) 2 ( both) 
P11 the time( in seconds)  that takes to let the motor kick in 
P12 acceleration strenght
P13 sensor type 
P14 control Amp limit 
P15 volatge limit ( cuts out when its hitting the named voltage) 
P16 reset the speedometer/ odometer
P17 cruise control 
P18 display adjustment 
P19 zero gear pedal powered or no pedal powered 
P20 comunicate with the controler

author: @middlemanclayton1
text:
Hey 👋

Sorry to inform but as of today 12/11/2022 the instructions given in your video to switch to throttle mode are incorrect, you need to:

1: Bike off
2: full throttle and front right brake lever (not both levers)
3: hold for 10 seconds (not 20)
4: and finished! Throttle is enabled

This worked on my A20+ that I got yesterday.

author: @garyhymes
text: I bought this bike 5 months ago and happy with it, for the price it's worth it. It still needs regular maintenance and tightening every month or so. One thing to note is that it's easy to disable the "Throttle mode" if you turn it on while accidentally holding down one of the breaks or accelerator. This can be confusing especially since the console provides no indication.  The battery will only last 30+ KM if you're using a lot of throttle mode only.

replies.author: @ChristopherMcglue

replies.author: @dtz1000
replies.text: ​​​@ChristopherMcglue Yes, you can make the pedal assist cut out at a higher speed.  Find the setting for wheel size and reduce the size of the wheel. This will increase your maximum pedal assist speed.  Use gps to check the speed as the speedometer will no longer be accurate.

replies.author: @juliusmutabaruka36
replies.text: The teacher explained it well! Make sure your bicycle is off, then Accelerate to maximum while touching all the breaks. Put it on and count 20 times, then release the breaks as well as the accelerator at the same time! There you are done. I did it after suffering for a year now! The vice versa is true if you want to activate the pedals!

author: @5p3ckyf0ur3y3d833k
text:
Throttle activation has changed:

Pull left brake and switch controller on and hold for 10 then hold the throttle and right brake for ten now switch off and on again. Assist level 3 and away you go.

Also: in 0 Assist if you press - it will travel at a max of 4.2 mph cruise control.

author: @binmanblog
text: Being able to make "changes" is not always a "hack" it is also about safety for a youngster on their first e-bike. Throttle control from start can help with stability so safer when starting off.

author: @rogerbraintree9552
text: I think I'd be more interested in electric propulsion if sodium ion batteries were used instead of lithium.  There is so much sodium around and obtaining sodium is not ecologically destructive as is mining for lithium.  Sodium can also be easily neutralised once the cycle life of the battery is over thus making them very easy to dispose of without the toxic hazards associated with lithium products.

author: @angelusmendez5084
text: Great vid! 👏

author: @RSmerlinRS
text:
should try change the voltage settings and make a 12s or 13s battery pack for it, wouldn't fit in the frame but you could have it in a backpack.

Take it out to a dirt path and see how fast it'll go under its own power

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate

replies.author: @Soruk42
replies.text: Video 1292 shows the motor itself is rated to 36V, so overvolting it might not be the best idea.

author: @jpsimon206
text: I'm interested in building a wheelchair based off of mountain bike hub motors. Ideally, it would be joystick driven. How do I go about getting the two motors to talk to each other and the joystick in such a way that they can interpolate say a diagonal input into the necessary speeds for each wheel. Is there a product on the market that would walk me through this? I don't have any coding abilities and I am not terribly skilled on a computer. I have no problem developing the mechanical platform. Is there a particular resource you can point me to?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text:
there seem to be plenty of motorised wheelchairs out there with dual drive mate. I found this product
https://www.uumotor.com/24v-36v-30a-electric-wheelchair-joystick-controller.html
this or something similar seems to be what you want

replies.author: @jpsimon206
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering thank you very very much for looking that up. I apologize, I should have been more specific. I own a chair similar to that now. I'm interested in building a chair that would allow me to go on mountain bike trails and hiking trails again. I believe I need the large wheels and gobs of torque to make it work. I'm also shooting for a somewhat more upright position where the body weight could influence steering. I'm sick of just being able to navigate cities. I hate cities. I want to make a new form of sport. The end goal is for it to be attractive to able-bodied people as well. I have a full machine shop at my disposal, I'm quite stupid when it comes to computers and modern signaling.

author: @McRootbeer
text: Please tell me you're going to try using this or some other off the shelf speed controller to power the magnetically assisted switched reluctance motor.  I'm dying to see how much better it can perform while being cheaper than an off the shelf electric motor.  You could try making the... stator? (The part that rotates and holds the metal bits that get attracted to the electromagnets) out of newspaper and casein glue composite for a cheaper/lighter? motor.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Now then a paper motor - interesting!

author: @roberthopgood1894
text: Mistake for the manufacturer to NOT INCLUDE a reset to defaults option

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: probably not mate - different country have different regs and a default reset might well negate some countries regulations and prevent import - that's probably why they updated the s866

replies.author: @roberthopgood1894
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering Easy enough to have location specific defaults
